What do you think about those two comments:
P1
Fast far-swaps are silently rewritten to adjacent moves
Verwerfen
endDrag now snaps any previewed target more than one tile away to an adjacent tile whenever the drag finishes within 150ms. That changes the actual move after the user has already targeted a valid distant destination, so teleport/free-swap tiles can no longer be used reliably with a quick flick. This is a gameplay regression, not just a UX hint, because the committed swap can differ from the previewed tile.


/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/input-handler.js:221-234
P2
Highlighted non-target tiles become interactive during drag
Verwerfen
updatePreviewState skips swap-dimmed for every merge-preview and unblock-preview tile, even when canPreviewSwap is false for that tile. Those tiles therefore regain pointer events during a drag. If the user moves onto one of these highlighted-but-invalid cells, the old valid preview stays active and releasing can execute that stale swap instead of cancelling or following the finger.


/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/input-handler.js:450-457


---

I think the first is fine right? This only happens if the target is 2 tiles away from the origin right? If it is further it does not snap back right.
I do not really understand P2

lastPrompt: What do you think about those two comments: P1 Fast far-swaps are silently rewritten to adjacent moves Verwerfen endDrag now snaps any previewed target more than one tile away to an adjacent tile when…

content.text:
Re P1: You're wrong about the distance. Condition is `manhattan > 1 && duration < 150ms` — applies to **any** distance > 1, not just 2. A teleport tile flicked across the whole board in <150ms gets snapped to adjacent. On mobile, a fast swipe across the screen in <150ms is easy.

So the regression is real: user sees teleport-tile preview on a far target → releases quickly → committed swap differs from the preview. Two options:
- Only snap when `manhattan === 2` (matches your mental model — fixes overshoot but preserves far-swap flicks).
- Skip snapping entirely when source is far-swap-capable (`isFarSwapCapable(tile)`).

Re P2: Trace at input-handler.js:450-457 — the dim rule exempts `isMerge` / `isUnblock` tiles so their highlight shows. But the exemption ignores whether the tile is itself a valid swap target.

Scenario:
1. Drag from source → valid far target T. Preview shows; T's matching tiles get `merge-preview`.
2. Those merge-preview tiles are **not** valid swap targets from source, but they're un-dimmed (interactive-looking).
3. Finger moves onto one of them. `updateDrag` calls `canPreviewSwap` → false → preview state not updated → `.preview` class still on T.
4. Release. `endDrag` finds `.gem.preview`, picks T, swaps with T — even though finger is elsewhere.

User's finger is on tile M, visual says M is active, but the swap that fires is the stale one on T.

Fix: in updatePreviewState, dim merge/unblock-preview tiles that aren't themselves swap-valid (i.e. drop the `!isMerge && !isUnblock` exemption, or gate it on `canPreviewSwap`).

Both comments are legit. P1 is the bigger one — it's a gameplay bug, not just UX.

content: Reviewing two code-review comments on input-handler.js drag UX. P1 (fast-flick snap rewrites far-swaps) is a real gameplay bug affecting any distance >1; P2 (merge-preview tiles stay interactive, stale swap fires) is also legit. Next: decide fix for P1.

message.content:
Hm ok. Lets try P2.
Thinking about P1: I think it is very unlikely that a user would do a far distance flick. 150ms are very fast. I think now user would do the flick this fast really. What do you think?

content.text:
Agree in practice. 150ms is genuinely fast — a deliberate far-swap with a teleport tile users would do more carefully (they want to see the destination). The common case is overshooting adjacent by 1, which is exactly what the snap fixes.

One edge case worth considering: `manhattan === 2` fast flicks. Someone with a teleport tile aiming 2 tiles away could realistically flick fast and get snapped to adjacent. Cheap mitigation: skip the snap when source is `isFarSwapCapable` — preserves intent for those tiles, keeps the overshoot fix for normal tiles. Small diff.

Want me to do that + P2?

content.text: The fix — drop the `!isMerge && !isUnblock` exemption. `.merge-preview` / `.unblock-preview` have `opacity: 1 !important` which overrides `.swap-dimmed`'s opacity, so the dashed highlight stays visible. What swap-dimmed adds is `pointer-events: none` — finger passes through invalid merge-preview tiles so they stop registering as drag targets.

+            // tiles keep their dashed highlight (opacity: 1 !important in CSS)

input.command: git diff main..HEAD -- src/input-handler.js src/animator.js | head -200

lastPrompt: We did break something. In the swap animation both tiles have a weird offset. One is like top-left off positioned, the other is then bottom-right off.  You can probably look at all the changes in this…

content.text:
Found it. Root cause is the new CSS `scale: 0.95` on `.gem.dragging` and `scale: 1.1` on `.gem.preview` — these use the separate `scale` property, not `transform: scale(...)`. `animateSwap` does `element.style.transform = translate(...)`, which does NOT override the `scale` property (they're independent now). And `getBoundingClientRect()` is measured **while the tiles are still scaled** — so the computed delta is wrong, and the scales stay during the animation.

Previously it worked because the old CSS used `transform: scale(...)` — the inline `transform` override wiped it.

Fix: clear those classes in `animateSwap` before measuring rects.

content.content: The file /Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/animator.js has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +        // which would skew getBoundingClientRect and leave the tiles scaled
+        // during the translate animation. Drop them before measuring.

content.text: Fix applied in `src/animator.js:14-19`. Note: the unused-import diagnostics are pre-existing, not related to my edit.
